title: IngestionSourceConfig
description: Defines how a data source connects to a workflow type and how its native format (JSON, CSV, DB rows) is mapped to WorkItem.fields. All source-format handling is in the ingestion adapter — the domain only ever receives a normalised WorkItem. The mapping is config-driven: no hardcoded column-to-field logic in code. A workflow type can have multiple active ingestion sources (e.g. Kafka AND a DB poll simultaneously) — this config references the workflow type; the WorkflowTypeDefinition does not enumerate its sources.

workflowType.description: All items from this source become work items of this workflow type.

sourceName.description: Human-readable identifier used in monitoring dashboards and error audit entries.

dbPollConfig.description: Required when sourceType=DB_POLL. Uses structured table reference (not raw SQL) to prevent SQL injection risks when config is user-editable.

dataSourceId.description: Reference to a registered JDBC datasource. Connection details managed outside this config.

schemaName.description: Database schema name. Null means default schema.

tableName.description: Table name. The adapter builds the query from this and watermarkColumn — no raw SQL accepted.

watermarkColumn.description: Column used for incremental polling (e.g. UPDATED_AT, SEQUENCE_ID).

processedFlagColumn.description: If set, the adapter updates this column to processedFlagValue after ingestion.

processedFlagValue.description: Value written to processedFlagColumn after ingestion (e.g. Y, true, 1).

delimiter.description: Column delimiter for CSV. Default: comma.

headerRow.description: Whether the first row contains column headers.

sheetName.description: XLSX only. Sheet to read. Null means the first sheet.

unknownColumnPolicy.description: How to handle source columns not present in fieldMappings. REJECT causes the entire file to be rejected.

fieldMappings.description: Maps source columns/paths to WorkItem.fields dot-notation paths. Nested target paths (e.g. counterparty.name) cause the adapter to build nested objects automatically from flat source columns. All source types use this same structure.

sourceColumn.description: Source column header (CSV/XLSX) or DB column name. Mutually exclusive with sourceJsonPath.

sourceJsonPath.description: JSON path for KAFKA sources with JSON/AVRO messages. Mutually exclusive with sourceColumn. If omitted for KAFKA, the full message payload maps to fields directly.

targetField.description: Dot-notation path in WorkItem.fields (e.g. trade.ref, counterparty.name, address.city). Must be declared in field-type-registry.fieldDeclarations for this workflowType.

required.description: If true and source value missing, the record is rejected and an error AuditEntry is written.

defaultValue.description: Used when value is absent and required:false.

transformation.description: Optional value transformation applied after reading. DATE_ISO8601 requires a sourceFormat hint in transformationConfig.

transformationConfig.description: Additional config for transformations that need parameters.

sourceFormat.description: For DATE_ISO8601: the source date format pattern (e.g. dd/MM/yyyy, yyyyMMdd).
